meta.description: 100 lightweight single-agent benchmark tasks for AgentGlue cache evaluation
meta.design: Tasks are ordered so later tasks naturally overlap with earlier ones. Running sequentially, AgentGlue cache hits should increase over time. Groups A-C establish baseline reads/searches; Groups D-G re-access the same resources with different questions, generating cache hits.

tasks.name: read runtime.py public methods
tasks.prompt: Read the file /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/runtime.py and count the number of public methods (methods not starting with underscore). List each one.
tasks.notes: First read of runtime.py; many later tasks re-read this file.

tasks.name: read dedup.py class names
tasks.prompt: Read the file /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/dedup.py and list all class names defined in it.
tasks.notes: First read of dedup.py; re-read in Groups D, E, F.

tasks.name: read shared_memory.py data flow
tasks.prompt: Read the file /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/shared_memory.py and explain the data flow from write to read.

tasks.prompt: Read the file /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/middleware/task_lock.py and identify all lock mechanisms used (mutex, semaphore, etc.).

tasks.prompt: Read the file /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/projects/AgentGlue/src/agentglue/core/allocator.py and describe the resource allocation strategy it implements.
